app.name: Firecrawl
app.bestFor: Turning any URL into clean, LLM-ready Markdown or schema-defined JSON in a single call — the default for RAG and AI pipelines.
app.rankBasis: Consistently ranks #1–2 across 2026 AI-scraping roundups (Bright Data, ScrapeOps, Apify comparisons); its open-source core is among the most-starred AI scrapers and it is the most-cited 'LLM-ready' scraper in AI-dev and r/webscraping discussion.
features: URL → clean Markdown/JSON in one call
pros: default choice for AI/RAG pipelines — clean, token-efficient output
simple API, very fast to integrate
open-source core plus a managed cloud
cons: credits don't roll over; extract/large crawls burn them fast
can struggle on the hardest anti-bot targets
costs climb quickly at scale

app.name: Bright Data
app.bestFor: Maximum-scale, hard-target scraping via the Unlocker API, Agent Browser and the broadest proxy infrastructure available.
app.rankBasis: Ranked top-2 for scale and hard-target scraping in nearly every 2026 roundup (its own blog, the DEV 30-tool test, imisofts); operates the industry's largest proxy network and has the strongest enterprise/G2 presence in the category.
app.pricingFree: Free trial + $25–50 credits; 5,000 free Web MCP requests/mo
app.pricingPaid: Pay-as-you-go from ~$1 per 1,000 requests (Unlocker/SERP/Crawl APIs); Web Scraper APIs ~$0.75–1 per 1k records
pros: unmatched scale and proxy infrastructure for hard targets
high success rate on heavily-protected sites
pay-for-success pricing with no monthly commitment
cons: complex product catalog and pricing
overkill and expensive for small jobs
steeper setup than plug-and-play tools

app.name: Apify
app.bestFor: A mature developer platform with 6,000+ prebuilt scrapers (Actors), scheduling and proxy management for complex, custom pipelines.
app.rankBasis: Top-3 platform in most 2026 developer roundups; its Actor marketplace (6,000+ prebuilt scrapers) is the largest of any scraping platform and it holds strong developer mindshare on G2 and r/webscraping.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: $5 platform credits/mo (no card required)
app.pricingPaid: Starter ~$39/mo — monthly platform credits; compute from ~$0.30/CU
pros: huge prebuilt scraper marketplace
flexible, mature developer platform
handles complex custom pipelines
cons: compute-unit billing is hard to predict
credits expire monthly (no rollover)
learning curve for non-developers

app.name: Browse AI
app.bestFor: No-code scraping and scheduled change-monitoring of any site for non-technical users.
app.rankBasis: The most-cited no-code scraper for non-technical users in 2026 roundups (Bright Data, Apify, imisofts); known for point-and-click 'robots' and scheduled change-monitoring, with a solid G2 review base.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: 50 credits/mo, up to 5 robots
pros: genuinely no-code, easy for non-technical users
strong site-monitoring / change-detection
large library of prebuilt templates
cons: credit model gets expensive at volume
struggles on complex, heavily-protected sites
less flexible than code-based tools

app.name: Octoparse
app.bestFor: A visual, point-and-click desktop tool that handles complex extraction with zero code.
app.rankBasis: A long-established visual desktop scraper still listed in 2026 no-code roundups (Bright Data, ScraperAPI free-tools); large historical user base and G2 reviews, though newer AI-native tools have eroded its lead.
app.pricingFree: Free plan (forever): 10 tasks, up to 50,000 rows/mo export
app.pricingPaid: Standard ~$99/mo billed yearly (listed $69–119 depending on source/billing)
pros: powerful visual scraping with zero code
handles complex multi-step extraction
prebuilt templates for popular sites
cons: desktop app with a real learning curve
pricing is steep and inconsistently listed
cloud features gated to higher tiers

app.name: Diffbot
app.bestFor: Enterprise AI extraction that returns structured entities from any page without per-site rules, backed by a knowledge graph.
app.rankBasis: The recognized enterprise 'AI extraction + knowledge graph' player, cited in 2026 extraction roundups (Bright Data, Firecrawl); niche but respected, with premium enterprise positioning and steady G2/Capterra coverage.
features: automatic AI entity extraction (no per-site rules)
pros: rule-free structured extraction across any page
unique knowledge-graph backing
strong for large-scale entity/data enrichment
cons: expensive — entry paid tier starts at $299/mo
overkill for simple scraping needs
smaller community than mainstream tools

app.name: Kadoa
app.bestFor: Self-healing AI extraction that adapts to layout changes, aimed at ongoing structured-data feeds.
app.rankBasis: A newer AI-native entrant with growing but still-limited mindshare; appears in 2026 AI-scraper reviews (Thunderbit, opentools) mainly for its self-healing extraction rather than as a roundup mainstay.
app.pricingPaid: Usage-based Flex plan (custom consumption pricing, no public per-credit rate) — Enterprise custom
features: self-healing AI extraction that adapts to layout changes
pros: self-healing extraction adapts automatically to site changes
context-based extraction (no rigid selectors)
good for continuous, monitored data feeds
cons: opaque, non-public pricing
smaller and less-proven than incumbents
limited independent review coverage

skill.name: Crawl4AI
skill.bestFor: The de-facto king of open-source AI web scraping (~74.8k★) — LLM-friendly crawler turning pages into clean Markdown/JSON for RAG and agent pipelines.
skill.rankBasis: ~74.8k★ (unclecode/crawl4ai) — one of the most-starred crawlers ever; hit #1 on GitHub Trending after its mid-2024 launch and is named the top OSS AI-scraping pick alongside Firecrawl in 2026 roundups (Firecrawl, Scrapfly).
pros: purpose-built for AI/RAG — clean, token-optimized output
fully OSS (Apache 2.0), self-host with no per-request cost
very active, fast-growing community
cons: younger project — API churn between releases
heavier than a plain parser (bundles browser automation)
overkill for simple static-HTML parsing

skill.name: Scrapy
skill.bestFor: The classic, most battle-tested production Python crawling framework (~63.4k★) — unmatched for large, structured crawls at scale.
skill.rankBasis: ~63.4k★, maintained by Zyte and estimated to power a large share of production scraping projects; named the production default for large-scale Python crawling in every 2026 roundup (Firecrawl, Scrapfly, Apify).
pros: battle-tested at scale for static-HTML crawls
complete batteries-included framework, not just a parser
huge mature community and extensive docs
cons: no native JS rendering — needs scrapy-playwright/Splash
steeper learning curve
Twisted foundation is at odds with the ecosystem's asyncio shift

skill.name: Playwright
skill.bestFor: Microsoft's cross-browser automation (~93k★) — the modern default for scraping JS-heavy, dynamically-rendered sites.
skill.rankBasis: ~93k★, backed by Microsoft; in 2026 widely declared the default recommendation for both E2E testing and browser-based scraping of JavaScript-heavy sites (Firecrawl, Scrapfly).
features: drives Chromium, WebKit & Firefox out of the box
pros: true cross-browser support
robust auto-waiting reduces flaky selectors
genuine first-class multi-language support (not just JS)
cons: heavy resource footprint (full browser per session)
an automation/testing tool — no built-in crawl queue/pipeline
browser-based scraping is slower and costlier at scale

skill.name: Puppeteer
skill.bestFor: Google's headless Chrome automation (~95k★) — ubiquitous and extremely mature for browser-driven scraping.
skill.rankBasis: ~95k★, maintained by the Chrome team — among the highest star counts in the category; 2026 comparisons still frame it as the right tool for Chrome-only automation despite Playwright's rise.
pros: mature, deeply Chrome-aligned API
leaner than Playwright for Chromium-only work
excellent for PDF/screenshot/stealth-scraping use cases
cons: no native WebKit/Safari; only experimental Firefox
JavaScript/TypeScript only — no official Python/Java/.NET
lacks native auto-waiting, so scripts are more flake-prone

skill.name: Beautiful Soup
skill.bestFor: The universally-adopted Python HTML/XML parser (beautifulsoup4, one of the most-downloaded packages ever) — the default for simple, static-page extraction.
skill.rankBasis: One of the most-downloaded Python packages ever (~200M+ monthly PyPI downloads for beautifulsoup4); the default recommendation for simple HTML parsing in essentially every 2026 Python-scraping guide (ZenRows, ScraperAPI, Firecrawl).
pros: extremely easy to learn with an intuitive API
handles malformed/messy HTML gracefully
near-universal tutorial coverage; pairs cleanly with Requests/HTTPX
cons: cannot render JavaScript-driven content
no built-in HTTP fetching, concurrency or async — parsing only
slower than lxml on large documents; no native XPath

skill.name: Scrapling
skill.bestFor: Fast-rising adaptive Python scraper (~71k★) that auto-heals when sites change and includes Cloudflare bypass — a genuine 2025-26 breakout.
skill.rankBasis: ~71k★ (D4Vinci/Scrapling); reached #1 on GitHub Trending in Feb 2026 — a genuine breakout — and is repeatedly flagged in 2026 roundups (The Web Scraping Club) as the most interesting adaptive/self-healing bet.
features: adaptive parser auto-relocates elements when structure changes
pros: self-healing selectors dramatically cut maintenance
out-of-the-box anti-bot handling without extra tooling
benchmarked as very fast among Python scrapers
cons: young project with a thinner production track record
adaptivity adds 'magic' that can be hard to debug
anti-bot claims still require real-world validation vs evolving defenses

skill.name: Crawlee
skill.bestFor: Apify's all-in-one Node.js scraping library (~25k★) wrapping Puppeteer/Playwright/Cheerio — the strongest JavaScript all-rounder.
skill.rankBasis: ~25k★, maintained by Apify; named the strongest all-in-one Node.js production default in 2026 roundups (Firecrawl, Scrapfly, Apify) and underpins most of the 19,000+ Actors in the Apify Store.
features: unified API over HTTP (Cheerio) & headless browsers (Playwright/Puppeteer)
pros: batteries-included, production-grade defaults out of the box
seamless switch between HTTP and browser crawling in one library
strong anti-blocking defaults and active corporate maintenance
cons: Node.js/TypeScript only (Python port is newer/less mature)
gravitational pull toward the paid Apify cloud ecosystem
heavier and more opinionated than lightweight alternatives

skill.name: Colly
skill.bestFor: The de-facto Go scraping framework (~25.4k★) — fast and idiomatic, the standard choice in the Go ecosystem.
skill.rankBasis: ~25.4k★, the de-facto standard Go scraping framework — named the Go production default in every 2026 roundup (Firecrawl, Scrapfly, HasData); some guides do flag its slower maintenance cadence.
pros: extremely fast and memory-efficient via Go's concurrency model
single-binary deployment makes it trivial to ship
clean, idiomatic API well-suited to concurrent crawling
cons: no JavaScript rendering — needs chromedp/Selenium for dynamic sites
smaller Go scraping ecosystem than Python/Node.js
maintenance cadence has drawn scrutiny in 2026 guides

skill.name: Scrapling Skill (Cedriccmh)
skill.bestFor: The most-starred standalone scraping Claude Code skill (~372★) — a SKILL.md wrapping Scrapling with automatic fetcher selection and Cloudflare bypass.
skill.rankBasis: ~372★ — one of the most-starred standalone (single-purpose) Claude Code scraping skills, rather than a skill buried in a large collection; its traction is largely downstream of the Scrapling library (~71k★) it wraps.
features: automatic fetcher selection via a decision tree
pros: auto-selects the right strategy — no Scrapling internals to learn
handles anti-bot, JS-rendered and authenticated sites out of the box
ships security guidance (credential isolation, prompt-injection awareness)
cons: niche adoption (~372★) next to mainstream tooling
entirely dependent on the external Scrapling library and Camoufox install
single-library focus — no fallback if Scrapling isn't the right fit

skill.name: Web Scraping skills (Mindrally)
skill.bestFor: Two genuine SKILL.md scraping skills (web-scraping + scrapy-web-scraping) inside a 240+ Claude Code skill collection (~197★).
skill.rankBasis: ~197★ — part of a large 240+ Claude Code skill collection (converted from Cursor rules); the two scraping skills are entries in that roster, so its standing comes from collection breadth rather than scraping depth, and its star count trails the dedicated Scrapling skill above.
features: web-scraping skill spanning static, dynamic & large-scale approaches
scrapy-web-scraping skill covering the full Scrapy lifecycle
pros: tool-agnostic advice covering many libraries
strong emphasis on ethical, robust practice
convenient if you already use Mindrally's broader collection
cons: scraping is a thin slice of a general 240+ skill dump
conventions/best-practices text, not executable tooling
lower adoption (~197★); no auto-fetcher or Cloudflare-bypass features

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt to rank by real adoption, not form factor (fixing the prior bug where a 78★ personal skill outranked Crawl4AI). Ranked by live GitHub stars and de-facto reputation: the recognized OSS scraping leaders (Crawl4AI, Scrapy, Playwright, Puppeteer, Beautiful Soup, Scrapling) lead as kind:repo, with the two most-adopted genuine SKILL.md scraping skills (Cedriccmh scrapling skill, Mindrally) below. Genuine Claude scraping skills top out in the low hundreds of stars, so they rank below the major libraries. Removed all MCP-server entries. Firecrawl OSS is omitted here as it already leads the App track. Star counts verified live 2026-07-24.
